title: Your First Program
description: Write a classic 'Hello World' program to get started.

slides.title: Hello, World! 👋
slides.content:
In programming, it's a tradition to make your very first program print the words **"Hello World!"** to the screen.

Let's see what a simple C++ program looks like!

slides.title: The Code 💻
slides.content: Here is the C++ code to print Hello World to the console.

slides.title: Printing Text 🖨️
slides.content:
To print text in C++, we use `std::cout`.

Think of `cout` as 'character output'. We use the insertion operator `<<` to send text to it.

Example:
`std::cout << "Welcome to Progly!";`

quizData.question: Which of these correctly prints text in C++?
quizData.explanation: `std::cout << "Hello";` is the correct C++ syntax for outputting text to the screen.

slides.title: The Semicolon ;
slides.content:
Did you notice the semicolon `;` at the end of `std::cout << "Hello World!";`?

In C++, **every statement must end with a semicolon**.
It's like the period at the end of an English sentence. Forgetting it is a very common mistake!

quizData.question: In C++, code statements do not need to end with any special character.
quizData.explanation: False! Every C++ statement MUST end with a semicolon (;).
